nodes.name: subshift
nodes.statement_text: closed shift-invariant subset of a full shift

nodes.name: shift of finite type
nodes.statement_text: subshift defined by a finite local syntax

nodes.name: sofic shift
nodes.statement_text: continuous shift-commuting factor of an SFT

nodes.name: topological entropy
nodes.statement_text: asymptotic growth rate of admissible patterns

nodes.name: right r.e. real
nodes.statement_text: real with a computable rational upper-approximation sequence

nodes.name: computable real
nodes.statement_text: real with a computable two-sided rational approximation sequence

nodes.name: irreducible subshift
nodes.statement_text: globally admissible far-apart patterns can be glued

nodes.name: factor map
nodes.statement_text: continuous surjection commuting with the shift action

nodes.name: Subshift (Hochman–Meyerovitch §2.1)
nodes.statement_latex: A closed shift-invariant subset $X\subseteq \Sigma^{\mathbb{Z}^d}$ is a $\mathbb{Z}^d$-subshift.
nodes.statement_text: A closed, shift-invariant subset of the full shift.

nodes.name: Shift of finite type (Hochman–Meyerovitch §2.1)
nodes.statement_latex: An SFT is defined by a finite alphabet $\Sigma$, a finite $F\subseteq \mathbb{Z}^d$ and a syntax $L\subseteq \Sigma^F$; the carrier is the set of $x\in\Sigma^{\mathbb{Z}^d}$ all of whose translated $F$-patterns lie in $L$.
nodes.statement_text: Subshift defined by a finite local syntax.

nodes.name: Locally admissible pattern (§2.1)
nodes.statement_latex: $a\in\Sigma^E$ is locally admissible for the syntax $L$ if $a|_{F+u}$ is congruent to a pattern in $L$ whenever $F+u\subseteq E$.
nodes.statement_text: Every translate of the syntax window contained in the domain matches some allowed pattern.

nodes.name: Globally admissible pattern (§2.1)
nodes.statement_latex: A finite pattern $a$ is globally admissible for $X$ if it appears in some $x\in X$.
nodes.statement_text: Pattern that occurs in some point of the subshift.

nodes.name: Irreducible SFT (§2.1)
nodes.statement_latex: An SFT $X$ is irreducible if there is a gap $r>0$ such that for every $A,B\subseteq\mathbb{Z}^d$ with $\|u-v\|_\infty\geq r$ for $u\in A,v\in B$ and every globally admissible $a\in\Sigma^A,\ b\in\Sigma^B$, the union $a\cup b$ is globally admissible.
nodes.statement_text: Far-apart globally admissible patterns can always be glued.

nodes.name: Topological entropy of a subshift (§2.2)
nodes.statement_latex: $h(X)=\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{1}{|F_n|}\log N_X(F_n)$ where $F_n=\{1,\dots,n\}^d$.
nodes.statement_text: Asymptotic growth rate of the number of admissible patterns on cubes.

nodes.name: Factor map / one-block map (§2.3)
nodes.statement_latex: A factor map $\varphi:X\to Y$ is a continuous surjection commuting with the shift action; equivalently it is a block code arising from $\varphi_0:\Sigma^F\to\Delta$. It is one-block if $|F|=1$.
nodes.statement_text: Continuous shift-commuting surjection.

nodes.name: Sofic shift (§2.3)
nodes.statement_latex: A subshift $Y$ is sofic if it is a factor of some SFT.
nodes.statement_text: Factor of a shift of finite type.

nodes.name: Shift-invariant Borel probability measure (§2.4)
nodes.statement_latex: $\mathcal{M}(X)$ denotes the set of $\sigma$-invariant Borel probability measures on $X$, with the weak-$*$ topology.
nodes.statement_text: Borel probability measure invariant under the shift action.

nodes.name: Measure-theoretic entropy h(μ) (§2.4, cited)
nodes.statement_latex: For $\mu\in\mathcal{M}(X)$, $h(\mu)$ denotes its Kolmogorov–Sinai entropy.
nodes.statement_text: Kolmogorov–Sinai entropy of a shift-invariant measure.

nodes.name: Computable real (§1)
nodes.statement_latex: $h\in\mathbb{R}$ is computable if there is an algorithm that, given $n\in\mathbb{N}$, produces a rational $r(n)$ with $|h-r(n)|<1/n$.
nodes.statement_text: Real with a computable rational approximation to arbitrary precision.

nodes.name: Right recursively enumerable real (§1)
nodes.statement_latex: $h\in\mathbb{R}$ is right recursively enumerable (upper semi-recursive) if there is a computable $r(n)\geq h$ with $r(n)\to h$, equivalently the right Dedekind cut $\{q\in\mathbb{Q}\mid q>h\}$ is recursive.
nodes.statement_text: Real with a computable monotone rational approximation from above.

nodes.name: Theorem 1.1 [MAIN]
nodes.statement_latex: For $d\geq 2$ the class of entropies of $d$-dimensional SFTs is exactly the class of non-negative right recursively enumerable real numbers.
nodes.statement_text: In dimension d ≥ 2, the entropies of SFTs are exactly the non-negative right r.e. reals.

nodes.name: Theorem 1.2 [MAIN]
nodes.statement_latex: For $d\geq 2$, the class of entropies of $d$-dimensional sofic shifts is the same as that of $d$-dimensional SFTs.
nodes.statement_text: For d ≥ 2 the entropy set of sofic shifts equals that of SFTs.

nodes.name: Theorem 1.3 [MAIN]
nodes.statement_latex: The topological entropy of a non-empty irreducible SFT is computable.
nodes.statement_text: Entropy of an irreducible SFT is a computable real.

nodes.name: Theorem 3.1 (= necessity half of 1.1)
nodes.statement_latex: For an SFT $X$ defined by syntax $L$ and $\tilde N_n=\#\{\text{locally admissible }F_n\text{-patterns}\}$, $\frac{1}{n^d}\log\tilde N_n\to h(X)$ from above; consequently $h(X)$ is right r.e.
nodes.statement_text: Locally admissible counts give a computable upper-approximation; SFT entropy is right r.e.

nodes.name: Theorem (sofic version of 3.1)
nodes.statement_latex: Let $Y$ be a sofic shift, factor of an SFT $X$ via the one-block map $\varphi_0$, and $\tilde M_n=\#\{\varphi(a):a\in W_n\}$. Then $\frac{1}{|F_n|}\log\tilde M_n\to h(Y)$ from above; consequently $h(Y)$ is right r.e.
nodes.statement_text: Sofic shifts admit a computable upper-approximation of entropy via image counts.

nodes.name: Corollary (sofic entropies are right r.e.)
nodes.statement_latex: The topological entropy of every sofic shift is right recursively enumerable.
nodes.statement_text: Every sofic shift entropy is right r.e.

nodes.name: Lemma 3.4 (irreducibility dichotomy)
nodes.statement_latex: Let $X$ be a non-empty irreducible SFT and $a\in\Sigma^{Q_k}$. Then exactly one of: (1) $a$ is not a sub-pattern of any locally admissible $b\in\Sigma^{Q_N}$ for large $N$ (so $a$ is not globally admissible); or (2) $a$ and every locally admissible $b\in\Sigma^{Q_N}$ are $\sqrt N$-compatible for large $N$ (so $a$ is globally admissible).
nodes.statement_text: For an irreducible SFT, every cube pattern either fails to extend or extends with compatibility on all large cubes.

nodes.name: Corollary 3.5 (decidable global admissibility)
nodes.statement_latex: For a non-empty irreducible SFT $X$ it is decidable whether a finite pattern $a$ is globally admissible.
nodes.statement_text: Global admissibility is decidable for nonempty irreducible SFTs.

nodes.name: Variational principle (cited, Misiurewicz 1976)
nodes.statement_latex: $h(X)=\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal{M}(X)}h(\mu)$, achieved at some equilibrium measure.
nodes.statement_text: Topological entropy equals the supremum of measure-theoretic entropies.

nodes.name: Upper semi-continuity of measure entropy (cited)
nodes.statement_latex: $\mu\mapsto h(\mu)$ is upper semi-continuous on $\mathcal{M}(X)$ with the weak-$*$ topology.
nodes.statement_text: Measure entropy is upper semi-continuous in the weak-* topology.

nodes.name: topEntropy_leftRE_irreducible (left-r.e. half of 1.3)
nodes.statement_latex: For a non-empty irreducible SFT, $h(X)$ is left recursively enumerable.
nodes.statement_text: Left-r.e. counterpart for irreducible SFTs.

nodes.name: computable_iff_leftRE_and_rightRE (Lean)
nodes.statement_latex: A real is computable iff it is both left and right recursively enumerable.
nodes.statement_text: Computable ⇔ left-r.e. ∧ right-r.e.

nodes.name: Open: is irreducibility necessary for computability?
nodes.statement_latex: Does $h(X)$ computable imply $X$ irreducible? (Theorem 1.3 gives one direction; the converse is open per §1.)
nodes.statement_text: Converse of Theorem 1.3; raised explicitly in §1 after Theorem 1.3.

nodes.name: Open: SFT cover of sofic shifts of equal entropy (d ≥ 2)
nodes.statement_latex: For $d\geq 2$, does every sofic shift have an SFT extension of the same entropy? (Coven–Paul covering theorem in $d=1$.)
nodes.statement_text: Multidimensional analog of the Coven–Paul covering theorem; partial result by Desai 2006.
